paragraphs.text: As an adult player, but since my childhood, I saw this media change during the 90s to the present day. Unfortunately, predatory practices (ubiquitous micro transactions, games of chance and virtual packs openings and seasonal passes) are common among major publishers’ productions (Ubisoft, Activision, Electronic Arts, Microsoft, Sony) sometimes even targeting young players (FIFA with PEGI 3 +, Fortnite PEGI 12 +). Secondly, with regard to preservation and the right to own a product, we have moved from a media mainly distributed on a physical medium (CD-rom, cartridges, DVDs, Blu-ray) to a market that is clearly pushed towards the digital world, allowing the same publishers to be able to revoke access to the games bought for download from one day to the next. Worse still, this year 2025 shows some examples of games released in physical format in the first place (Blu-ray on Xbox series and PS5) but ultimately containing no data from the game (here I talk about Indiana Jones and the old circle, but also Doom the dark ages). This kind of Trojan horse allows the end user to be deceived by selling a Blu-ray, but contains only a download key. This creates physical products with a limited duration, since, in the same way as a game bought for download, his licence can be revoked when the editor so wishes. On this occasion, the people who bought a physical game thought to be the owner of the game.
